label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if an 8×8 chessboard with two diagonally opposite corners removed can be completely covered by 31 dominoes. Each domino covers two adjacent squares. Let me think about this step by step. First, I remember that a regular 8×8 chessboard has 64 squares. If we remove two corners that are diagonally opposite, that leaves us with 62 squares. Since each domino covers two squares, 31 dominoes would indeed cover all 62 squares. But the question is whether such a tiling is possible. I recall that domino tiling problems often involve colorings of the chessboard. A standard chessboard is colored with alternating black and white squares. Each domino, when placed on the board, will always cover one black and one white square. So, if the number of black and white squares is equal, then maybe a tiling is possible. Let me check the color of the two removed corners. The chessboard has an equal number of black and white squares.
